title: A 'Malcolm & Marie' Meltdown With John David Washington and Sam Levinson. Plus: 'The Sound of Music,' Finally.
format: A Christopher Plummer tribute (with Sean finally watching The Sound of Music), then Sean and Amanda fight about Malcolm & Marie before its makers show up to answer for it.

segments.heading: Remembering Christopher Plummer
segments.note: and a first watch of The Sound of Music

films.title: The Sound of Music
films.note: Plummer as Captain von Trapp, the role that made him and the one he spent a career pushing against. Sean finally watched it this weekend and found Maria a mystifying lead; Amanda grew up on it.

films.title: Beginners
films.note: His Oscar came here, playing Ewan McGregor's father, who comes out late in life. Mike Mills film Sean loves.

films.title: The Insider
films.note: Michael Mann's 60 Minutes drama. Plummer plays Mike Wallace without impersonating him. 'Who told your chubby little fingers they could edit me?'

films.title: The Silent Partner
films.note: A clever Canadian bank-heist thriller. Plummer is one of the most evil men Sean's seen on screen.

films.title: Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
films.note: A Klingon general who quotes Shakespeare; better than it has any right to be.

films.title: Murder by Decree
films.note: Bob Clark's Sherlock Holmes, played more open-hearted than the usual preening addict.

films.title: The Last Station
films.note: Plummer as Tolstoy; his first Oscar nomination, in his late 70s.

films.title: Inside Man
films.note: Spike Lee heist movie Sean rewatches constantly. Plummer playing in the Sound of Music shadow.

films.title: Malcolm X
films.note: A small role in the Spike Lee world.

films.title: Knives Out
films.note: The murdered patriarch; a late-career classic.

films.title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
films.note: David Fincher; not a 9 a.m. rewatch.

films.title: All the Money in the World
films.note: Replaced Kevin Spacey at the last minute and got an Oscar nomination for it.

films.title: The New World
films.note: Terrence Malick. Great directors liked him and he liked them.

segments.heading: A 'Malcolm & Marie' Meltdown
segments.note: Sean and Amanda on the Netflix two-hander

films.title: Malcolm & Marie
films.note: Levinson's quarantine-shot two-hander, black-and-white, Zendaya and John David Washington fighting through a night after his premiere. Critics savaged it; both hosts liked it. The knock: it detours from the relationship into film-criticism monologues, and a white writer routes them through a Black director.

films.title: Assassination Nation
films.note: Levinson's prior film, and the source of the movie: he forgot to thank his wife at its Sundance premiere. The LA Times critic Malcolm rages at is widely read as its reviewer.

segments.heading: The makers answer for it
segments.guest: Sam Levinson & John David Washington on

films.title: Malcolm & Marie
films.note: Written by Levinson at Zendaya's behest when Euphoria shut down; he read Washington the first pages aloud over the phone, playing both parts. Shot handheld after they threw out the first days of dailies.

films.title: Pieces of a Woman
films.note: Washington's last-great-watch. The birth sequence 'was insane.'

films.title: Mrs. Miniver
films.note: Levinson had been rewatching a piece of it the night before.

films.title: The Graduate
films.note: Levinson, astounded by the editing: the ten seconds of black after Benjamin shuts the hotel-room door.

Ads: Netflix FIFA World Cup Launch Edition
